project.name: sia-agent
project.description: SIA is a Self-Improving AI framework that coordinates meta, target, and feedback agents to iteratively improve task-specific AI systems on benchmark tasks. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Docs & Project Setup
layers.description: Repository documentation, package metadata, CI workflows, environment configuration, and static task assets.

layers.name: CLI & Run Orchestration
layers.description: Command parsing and the generation loop that sets up runs, executes target agents, evaluates outputs, and triggers feedback.

layers.name: Agent Backends & Providers
layers.description: Provider, profile, API-key, agent-reference, and backend adapter code that connects SIA to external model runtimes.

layers.name: Prompting & Context
layers.description: Prompt construction and generation-history context that feed the meta and feedback agents.

layers.name: Task Benchmarks
layers.description: Built-in benchmark task packages, reference agents, evaluation scripts, and task datasets.

layers.name: Web Dashboard
layers.description: FastAPI server and static web assets for inspecting SIA runs, generations, artifacts, and trajectories.

layers.name: Tests & Golden Fixtures
layers.description: Pytest coverage and golden prompt/context fixtures that lock down the orchestration, providers, CLI, tasks, and web APIs.

layers.name: Supporting Files
layers.description: Additional source, data, and configuration files not captured by the primary architectural groupings.

tour.title: Start With the Project Contract
tour.description: Read the README and package metadata to understand SIA's self-improving agent loop, install options, dependencies, and console entry point.

tour.title: Follow the Run Lifecycle
tour.description: Trace CLI parsing into the orchestrator, then through run setup, generation execution, target evaluation, and feedback handoff.

tour.title: Inspect Configuration and Providers
tour.description: Profiles and provider definitions determine which agent implementation, model, credentials, and target reference are used for each role.

tour.title: Understand Agent Execution Backends
tour.description: The backend adapters implement the shared run-agent interface for Claude, OpenHands, and Pydantic AI execution paths.

tour.title: Read Prompting and Context
tour.description: Prompt builders and the context manager encode task files, run history, execution results, and feedback into inputs for the meta and improvement agents.

tour.title: Explore Built-In Tasks
tour.description: The bundled task directories combine public/private data, evaluation scripts, reference agents, and sample task descriptions for SIA experiments.

tour.title: Use the Web and Tests to Verify Behavior
tour.description: The web layer exposes run artifacts for inspection, while the tests and golden fixtures verify CLI behavior, generation flow, prompts, providers, and task structure.
